factors.name: Macro data gap
factors.summary: No fresh inflation (CPI/PCE), Fed, or NFP data in last 24h. Prior June jobs/inflation data mentioned as 'worse than many realize' and 'bullish for bonds' per MarketWatch, suggesting softer labor market and easing inflation trajectory — typically supportive for growth equities and duration assets, but data is stale.

factors.name: Deregulatory backdrop
factors.summary: Trump administration proposing 702 regulatory rollbacks per news. Broad deregulation favors megacap tech and financials, potentially supporting NDX multiple expansion over medium term.

factors.name: Real yields & duration sensitivity
factors.summary: No new Treasury or TIPS data in window. Lack of fresh nominal/real yield signals makes duration positioning unclear; tech is highly sensitive to real rate moves but no fresh catalyst to reprice.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings calendar
factors.summary: No AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, TSLA, or META earnings announced in last 24h news window. Earnings season forward calendar unknown from provided sources; lack of near-term catalyst.

factors.name: AI capex & hyperscaler guidance
factors.summary: No new AWS, Azure, GCP capex guidance or NVDA demand commentary in window. Crypto-related tokenization and prediction market activity (Kalshi, Kraken) mentioned but unrelated to core hyperscaler capex trends.

factors.name: Geopolitical & policy noise
factors.summary: Multiple headlines on Trump-Putin-Ukraine dialogue, Iran ceasefire effects (oil supply glut, tanker disruptions), North Korea tests, Venezuela quake — all create macro uncertainty but no direct Nasdaq catalyst. Oil weakness (peace deal) is mildly positive for broad equities via lower energy costs.

factors.name: Crypto correlation & risk-on sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin rebounded above $63K and extends relief rally; BTC deposit activity shows renewed dip-buying and recovery sentiment. BTC-NQ correlation typically risk-on; modest bullish tone but not dominant enough to override lack of tech-specific catalysts.

factors.name: Recent price action — 4h vs 24h
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.44% in last 4h but -1.24% over 24h. Intraday bounce contradicted by larger downtrend; suggests exhaustion or choppy consolidation after recent selling, typical of PHASE_2 (move in progress, lack of conviction).

factors.name: Antitrust & regulatory risks
factors.summary: No new DOJ/FTC/EU DMA actions on AAPL, GOOGL, MSFT in this window, but deregulation theme and AI regulation discussion (Perplexity, EU prediction market restrictions) remain modest headwinds for platform risk.

factors.name: Macro uncertainty ahead
factors.summary: No major data releases announced in next 48-72h per window, but upcoming earnings season and potential FOMC guidance await; holiday (July 4) may create thin liquidity and choppy mean reversion.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is trapped in a cautious consolidation after a 1.24% decline over the past 24 hours, with a modest intraday recovery (+0.44% in 4h) suggesting potential early dip-buying. The macroeconomic backdrop is mixed: softer June labor data and easing inflation narrative support long-duration growth assets, while geopolitical noise (Iran peace, Trump-Putin talks, North Korea tests) and oil supply glut create cross-currents. Mega-cap tech earnings and hyperscaler capex guidance—the two largest NDX drivers—remain absent from the news cycle, leaving the index without a fresh catalyst to break decisively in either direction. Bitcoin's recovery above $63K hints at renewed risk-on appetite, but the move is not yet translating into conviction in equities.
catalysts_ahead: Next inflation print (CPI/PCE timing TBD, but typically mid-month)
FOMC meeting (next scheduled date TBD; if imminent within 72h, will suppress volatility)
Mega-cap earnings calendar: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, TSLA, META (exact dates not in window; assume late July or August)
Treasury auction and real yield repricing
Week of July 7-11 likely to see earnings guidance updates and forward capex commentary from hyperscalers
screening.risk_warning: Fresh inflation surprise (CPI >3.5% print) or hawkish Fed signal would invalidate bullish bias and crush duration multiples, likely pushing NAS100 down 2–4%. Conversely, unexpectedly dovish inflation data or new hyperscaler capex acceleration would flip to bullish. No major catalyst in next 48h means range-bound chop is likely; avoid aggressive directional bets until earnings or data clarity emerges.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in choppy PHASE_2 consolidation with no clear catalyst. Avoid aggressive entries; wait for either (a) retest of 29,100–29,200 support with bullish reversal signal (pivot confirmation, volume confirmation), or (b) earnings/inflation data release to establish clearer directional bias. If trading intraday, favor mean-reversion scalps within 29,200–29,600 band rather than breakout trades.

factors.name: Recent price action
factors.summary: 24h change of -1.24% shows downward pressure despite modest +0.44% 4h bounce. Suggests weakness has momentum but not yet capitulated.

factors.summary: No new Fed communication or rate decision in news window. Trump-Putin dialogue and Iran-US peace deal may influence long-term policy but no immediate catalyst.

factors.summary: No DXY or FX data in news. USD direction critical for multinational tech but no clarity.

factors.summary: Iran-US peace deal and Trump-Putin dialogue noted, but no Taiwan/chip export control escalation. Geopolitical risk slightly reduced.

factors.name: Sentiment / market internals
factors.summary: Investors shifting to India for shelter from AI-driven volatility (per Bloomberg 2026-07-05T02:00), suggesting concern about tech/growth valuations and rotation risk.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a post–July 4th holiday-thinned tape with conflicting signals. Crypto and risk assets have bounced (BTC >$63k, equities +0.44% in 4h), but the 24h -1.24% decline and Bloomberg's report of investors fleeing to India suggest underlying unease about AI-driven valuations and growth multiple sustainability. With no major macro releases or mega-cap earnings in the window, conviction is low. The market appears to be in a shallow retest phase rather than a sustained recovery.
catalysts_ahead: US weekly jobless claims (if scheduled for next business day)
FOMC minutes or Fed speaker commentary
Mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, AMZN, META dates TBD)
Next CPI / PCE print (timing unclear from this news cycle)
Hyperscaler AI capex commentary (AWS/Azure/GCP earnings calls)
Any antitrust development on EU/DOJ tech investigations
screening.risk_warning: If Bitcoin and risk assets reverse sharply below $60k, or if the next CPI print comes in hot (triggering Fed hawkishness), NQ100 could accelerate lower. Conversely, strong earnings beats and dovish Fed signals could rapidly shift bias BULLISH. Holiday liquidity and low volume reduce reliability of technical signals—be cautious of whipsaws until post-holiday trading normalizes. Investor rotation to India/emerging markets (noted in news) suggests tech/growth may continue to underperform if growth concerns persist.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in a shallow bounce (Phase 2); do not chase. Wait for a retest of support near 29100–29200 or a break and close above 29500 with confirmation (volume, breadth, or macro catalyst) before initiating fresh longs. Shorts on any relief rally toward 29600 if no earnings catalyst materializes.

factors.name: Macro data vacuum post-July 4
factors.summary: No major US economic data released in last 24h; market in holding pattern after US Independence Day. Next critical data (NFP, CPI) likely early-to-mid week.

factors.name: Foxconn Q2 revenue +40% YoY
factors.summary: Supply-chain positive for Apple ecosystem and semiconductor demand narrative. Suggests manufacturing momentum feeding into NDX hardware/cloud cycle.

factors.name: BOK warns of leveraged ETF volatility risks (Samsung, SK Hynix)
factors.summary: Central bank concern about single-stock leverage amplifying volatility in semiconductors. Indirect warning on concentration risk in tech/chip names that comprise NDX.

factors.name: Geopolitical (Iran, Venezuela, Taiwan)
factors.summary: Khamenei death, Hormuz shipping tensions, Taiwan military posture, Venezuela earthquake damage. Risk-off backdrop; potential supply-chain and energy disruption concerns.

factors.name: Bitcoin rebound to $63k+ range
factors.summary: BTC rallying above $63k suggests risk-on sentiment returning. NQ-BTC correlation positive on risk-on days; crypto strength often precedes tech equity strength.

factors.name: Real yields / Treasury curve
factors.summary: No fresh US Treasury data in window; curve inferred stable post-July 4. Long-duration growth (tech) pricing in modest real yield expectations, but no new catalyst.

factors.name: India equities gaining as AI hedge
factors.summary: Investors rotating to India to shelter from AI-driven US market swings. Suggests some hedging activity out of NDX but not a directional call on NQ itself.

factors.name: Price action: +0.44% 4h, -1.24% 24h
factors.summary: Intraday bounce (+0.44% in 4h) but still down 1.24% on the day. Mixed signal; move is modest and lacks conviction; consolidating below recent highs.

factors.name: Antitrust/regulatory risks (implicit)
factors.summary: EU DMA, US DOJ antitrust actions against mega-caps remain in background. No fresh headlines but structural regulatory overhang for AAPL, GOOGL, META, MSFT persists.

factors.name: Earnings calendar silence
factors.summary: No mega-cap tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, META, TSLA) announced or imminent in this 24h window. Summer lull; next big catalysts likely mid-July.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is consolidating post-July 4 holiday with mixed cross-currents: positive Foxconn supply-chain signal and Bitcoin recovery (+0.44% 4h) offset by geopolitical risks (Iran, Taiwan, Venezuela) and central bank warnings on leveraged ETF volatility in semiconductor names. The 1.24% 24h decline suggests modest weakness despite intraday bounce; lack of major US data and absence of mega-cap earnings guidance creates a vacuum. Sentiment is cautiously risk-on (BTC strength) but constrained by regulatory overhang and valuation sensitivity to real yields, which remain in flux pending next CPI/NFP releases.
catalysts_ahead: CPI release (early-to-mid week, high impact on real yields and growth multiple compression)
FOMC speakers / Fed communications (could reshape rate expectations)
Mega-cap tech earnings calendar (likely mid-late July: TSLA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA)
Earnings guidance on hyperscaler cloud capex and AI demand trends
Taiwan/China developments (military posture, chip export controls)
Iran geopolitical resolution or escalation (energy, shipping, risk-off/on sentiment)
screening.risk_warning: A sharp CPI surprise or Fed hawkish pivot would compress growth multiples and trigger NDX selloff; conversely, dovish Fed surprise or cool inflation data could reignite the AI/tech rally. Additionally, escalation in Iran tensions or Taiwan straits could trigger broad risk-off, dragging NQ down. The absence of mega-cap earnings guidance leaves the market vulnerable to data-driven repricing. Finally, if BOK-style central bank warnings about leveraged ETF risks spread globally, it could trigger a reduction in single-name tech leverage and cascade into redemptions.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is modest and consolidating; wait for a clearer directional break above 29,500 (resistance) or below 29,050 (support) before committing. Next major macro catalyst (CPI or FOMC signal) will likely provide the catalyst; until then, treat as neutral-to-choppy chop. If buying, look for pullback to 29,150–29,250 on any intraday dip; if selling, use resistance at 29,500 as a short trigger with tight stops.
